title: Marauder Starjacker

{{Quote|Ore pirates, Captain Gruna! They're attaching to the hull!|A crewmember of Gruna's ore hauler, during the ''Marauder Starjacker''{{'s}} attack|<ref name="nomi2">[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]]</ref>}}
The '''''Marauder Starjacker''''' was a modified [[E-2 Asteroid Miner]] [[Captain/Legends|captained]] by [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Finhead Stonebone]] in the [[Galactic Standard Calendar|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Originally an ordinary [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''Starjacker'' and its sister ship, the ''[[Stenness Raider]]'', were captured some time before [[3999 BBY]] by [[Finhead Stonebone's pirate gang|Stonebone's pirate gang]] and modified extensively to make them more suitable for [[Ore/Legends|ore]] raiding. In 3999 BBY the ''Marauder Starjacker'' was involved in an attempted [[Attack on the Ithullan ore hauler|attack]] on an [[Gruna's ore hauler|ore hauler]] commanded by the [[Nessie]] captain [[Gruna]]. The ''Marauder Starjacker'' destroyed the hauler's defenses and began to siphon the ship's valuable [[mutonium]] ore, only to be captured by the [[Enforcer One (dreadnaught)|''Enforcer One'']], the [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who was protecting the [[Ithullan ore hauler|ore hauler]]. The [[Prisoner/Legends|captured]] pirates were brought before Bogga on [[Vo Dasha]], where Bogga enlisted Stonebone to go to the desolate [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] in order to retrieve a lost shipment of [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] from Bogga's sworn enemy, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. Stonebone, along with a gang of pirates and Hutt [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcers]], went to Ambria in the ''Marauder Starjacker''; however, they were [[Skirmish on Ambria|defeated]] by Thon and his [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|trainee]], [[Nomi Sunrider]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Dreebo to ''Enforcer One''… we've got two ore-suckers on the whitebeam run.|Dreebo|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
[[File:MarauderStarjacker_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|180px|''Starjacker'' schematics]]
The ''Marauder Starjacker'' was an [[E-2 Asteroid Miner]] manufactured by [[Byblos Drive Yards/Legends|Byblos Drive Yards]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] had mechanical pincers that could grapple [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] and [[Plasma drill/Legends|plasma drills]] that could blow them into smaller chunks, alongside suction tubes that could suck up what remained, which made it an excellent [[Mining/Legends|mining]] vessel. However, as the ship was under the ownership of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Finhead Stonebone]], the ''Starjacker'' was used for criminal purposes, particularly siphoning [[Ore/Legends|ore]] from ore-hauling ships. To this end, the pincers were used to grab and immobilize targets, the drills used to rip open the [[Hull/Legends|hull]], and the tubes to suck out the ore. The ''Marauder Starjacker'' measured out to be 110 meters long, and could carry 200 tons of cargo. To increase his chance of success in his raids, Stonebone replaced the ''Starjacker''{{'s}} normal [[Engine/Legends|engines]] with [[Corellia StarDrive]] [[microthruster]]s.<ref name="companion" /> These were not the only modifications that Stonebone made to the ship; after taking possession of the ''Starjacker'', his [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] moved the plasma drills to the front of the ship to use as weapons, with a trio of [[Power generator/Legends|power generators]] feeding each drill. Also, the prismatic drill [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] were modified to give the weapons a longer range. Although the drills lacked the range of [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], they were more than powerful enough to punch through the hulls of [[Ithullan ore hauler]]s.<ref name="egvv">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

Stonebone made several other modifications to the layout of the ship as well. His engineers reinforced the tips of the ''Starjacker''{{'s}} digging claws with [[monomolecular slicer]]s, making the claws capable of slicing through thick armor plating. He also removed the ship's filtering system, as the ship would only be sucking in pure ore—this action also increased the ship's cargo space by a third. Another modification made to the ''Marauder Starjacker'' was the removal of the ship's mining stations, allowing for enough space to contain more than one hundred raiders for boarding missions. Two [[Maneuvering jet/Legends|maneuvering thrusters]] were located on top of the ship near the engines at the rear. The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] was directly at the front of the ''Starjacker'', with a rectangular-shaped [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]].<ref name="egvv" />

==History==
{{Quote|Start suckin' up the ore!|Finhead Stonebone|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
The ''Marauder Starjacker'' and her sister ship, the ''[[Stenness Raider]]'', began service as ordinary E-2 mining vessels. When [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Finhead Stonebone set out to begin raiding Ithullan ore haulers, made from the carapaces of [[Colossus wasp/Legends|colossus wasps]], he knew that E-2s could be useful, so he decided to obtain some to help with his ore-raiding endeavor. Stonebone subsequently attacked a mining operation near the [[Varl system/Legends|Varl system]] and stole both the ''Marauder Starjacker'' and the ''Stenness Raider''. His engineers then modified them extensively to make them more suitable for ore raiding.<ref name="egvv" /> Both E-2s were then used in raids on the ore haulers. In [[3999 BBY]], the ''Marauder Starjacker'' was involved in an attempted [[Attack on the Ithullan ore hauler|attack]] on one particular [[Gruna's ore hauler|Ithullan ore hauler]], commanded by the [[Nessie]] captain [[Gruna]]. Although the vessel was protected by local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], the ''Starjacker'' had no problem reaching the hauler and attaching to its hull. One of Bogga's [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcers]], [[Dreebo]], attacked the ''Marauder Starjacker'' in his [[light tactical fighter]], but a well-placed shot by Stonebone's [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] [[C'borp]] destroyed the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]].<ref name="nomi2" />

[[File:FinheadStonebone.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Finhead Stonebone, captain of the ''Marauder Starjacker'']]
Finding the ore hauler defenseless, the ''Marauder Starjacker'' went to work, puncturing the ship's hull. The ''Starjacker''{{'s}} suction tubes were deployed to suck up the valuable [[mutonium]] ore, while a group of boarding troops, including Stonebone and his [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]] [[Roko]], descended down into the gutted ore hauler to take possession of what remained. However, their success was short-lived; Bogga's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] [[Enforcer One (dreadnaught)|''Enforcer One'']], appeared and caught both the ''Marauder Starjacker'' and the ''Stenness Raider'' in its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]. The ships were brought into the ''Enforcer One''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], where Bogga's enforcer [[Grimorg]] took the pirates [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]]. The pirates were brought to [[Bogga's palace|Bogga's estate]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Vo Dasha]], where Bogga brought the marauders before him. Bogga enlisted the pirates to travel to the desolate [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] to retrieve a lost shipment of [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] from Bogga's sworn enemy, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. Bogga sent a contingent of his own men to accompany Stonebone and the pirates, and the group took the ''Marauder Starjacker'' and the ''Stenness Raider'' to the desolate [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], where Thon waited. The pirate and enforcer gang parked the ships in low [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], and took [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barges]] onto the planet's surface. However, in a [[Skirmish on Ambria|confrontation]] with Thon and his [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainee]] [[Nomi Sunrider]], the group was decimated and defeated, and the few survivors forced to flee.<ref name="nomi3">[[Tales of the Jedi 5|''Tales of the Jedi'' 5]]</ref>

==Commanders and crew==
{{Quote|That's it, cap'n! We're all ''rich!''|Roko|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
The captain of the ''Marauder Starjacker'' was the pirate Finhead Stonebone. Stonebone ordered the many modifications that were performed on the ''Starjacker'' in order to make it a more suitable raiding ship.<ref name="egvv" /> Stonebone was assisted on the ''Marauder Starjacker'' by his first mate and good friend Roko, who often joined the boarding team which descended into targeted ships.<ref name="nomi2" /> The ''Marauder Starjacker'' had a crew of fourteen, with four gunners:<ref name="companion" /> one of these was C'borp, Stonebone's chief gunner.<ref name="nomi2" /> The ''Marauder Starjacker'' also maintained a team of more than 100 raiders reserved for boarding missions.<ref name="companion" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Marauder Starjacker'' first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Saga of Nomi Sunrider]]'', the second story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released over late [[1993]]–early [[1994]]. The ''Starjacker'' appeared in both the [[Tales of the Jedi 4|second]]<ref name="nomi2" /> and [[Tales of the Jedi 5|third and final]] issues of the arc.<ref name="nomi3" /> In these appearances the ''Marauder Starjacker'' was illustrated by [[David Roach]].<ref name="nomi2" /><ref name="nomi3" /> Further information on the ''Marauder Starjacker'' was given in [[Bill Smith]]'s [[1996]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''<ref name="egvv" /> and later that same year in [[George R. Strayton]]'s ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''.<ref name="companion" /> The ship was also mentioned in an [[The Horse's Mouth|article]] in [[Star Wars Insider 26|''Star Wars Insider'' 26]],<ref name="horsesmouth">'{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}}</ref> and garnered entries in the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="swe">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'',<ref name="agswu">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>
